definition: The KB's quantified base rates (yield curve 7/7, concentration 3/3, steepening trap 5/7) rest on samples of N=3-9 with systematically unreported confidence intervals. Wilson confidence intervals reveal ranges far wider than the point estimates imply, making KB confidence ratings 1-2 points too high.
evidence: Wilson CI for yield curve 7/7 is [65-100%]
Wilson CI for steepening trap 5/7 is [36-92%]
KB assigns confidence 7-8 to claims on N=3-9 observations
Pair_2 and Pair_3 debates confirmed as strongest consensus finding
open_questions: What minimum sample size would be required for the precision levels implied by the KB?
Can cross-country data expand effective sample sizes for any of these base rates?
